name: PBD-2025-05 — Modern Slavery Tender Clauses
description: Procurement Board Direction requiring mandatory modern slavery tender clauses in approach-to-market documents and contracts where the procurement involves overseas supply chains or high modern slavery risk supply categories. Updated February 2026. Triggered by the 'overseas' overlay or high-risk categories (garments, electronics, cleaning, food, construction materials). Requires suppliers to complete a modern slavery risk assessment and cooperate with audits.
source: NSW Procurement Board Direction PBD-2025-05 (updated February 2026); Modern Slavery Act 2018 (NSW); NSW Procurement Policy Framework

pre_checks.title: Assess modern slavery risk in the supply chain
pre_checks.body: Before publishing approach-to-market documents, assess whether the procurement involves overseas supply chains or high modern slavery risk categories. High-risk categories include: garments and textiles, electronics, cleaning services, food products, construction materials, and agricultural products. Document the risk assessment outcome in the procurement file.

pre_checks.title: Confirm mandatory clauses are included in tender documents
pre_checks.body: Verify that the mandatory modern slavery tender clauses (as specified in PBD-2025-05 and the NSW Procurement Policy Framework Annex) are included in all approach-to-market documents before publication. Do not publish a tender without these clauses where the Direction applies.

obligations.title: Include mandatory modern slavery clauses in tender documents
obligations.body: Where the procurement involves overseas supply chains, mandatory modern slavery tender clauses must be included in all approach-to-market documents (RFQ, RFT, EOI). Suppliers must be required to complete a modern slavery risk assessment as part of their tender response.

obligations.title: Include modern slavery contract clauses requiring supplier cooperation
obligations.body: Contracts must include modern slavery clauses requiring the supplier to: (1) cooperate with the agency's modern slavery risk assessments; (2) permit and cooperate with audits of the supply chain; (3) notify the agency of any identified modern slavery risk or instance; and (4) take remediation action where directed.

obligations.title: Document modern slavery risk assessment in procurement file
obligations.body: A modern slavery risk assessment must be completed and retained in the procurement file for all procurements to which PBD-2025-05 applies. The assessment must identify supply chain risks, the steps taken to address them, and how the mandatory clauses address the identified risks.

obligations.title: Report modern slavery actions in agency Annual Report
obligations.body: Agencies with annual turnover ≥ $50M must report on actions taken to address modern slavery risks in their supply chains in the Annual Report. Procurement actions under PBD-2025-05 should be captured in this reporting.

pathway_overrides.if_arrangement_exists: Where an approved WoG arrangement covers the procurement, confirm that the arrangement's standard contract already incorporates modern slavery clauses meeting PBD-2025-05 requirements. If not, add the mandatory clauses via a special conditions schedule.
pathway_overrides.if_no_arrangement: Include mandatory modern slavery tender clauses in all approach-to-market documents before publication. Require suppliers to complete the modern slavery risk assessment template as part of their tender response.

steps.text: Complete a modern slavery risk assessment for this procurement. Document whether the supply chain involves overseas sourcing or high-risk categories (garments, electronics, cleaning, food, construction materials).

Confirm that mandatory modern slavery tender clauses (per PBD-2025-05) are included in approach-to-market documents before publication. Include the supplier modern slavery risk assessment template as a required tender response document.

approval_additions.note: Confirm modern slavery risk assessment is complete and mandatory PBD-2025-05 clauses are incorporated in tender and contract documents before publication and execution.
